title: Smoky Roasted Irish Salmon Gratin
description: A rich and flavorful Irish-inspired dish featuring smoky salmon, creamy potatoes, and a crunchy crust.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the brown sugar, smoked paprika, and a pinch of salt.
Brush the salmon fillets with the sugar mixture, making sure to get some under the skin as well.
Place the salmon fillets on the prepared baking sheet, skin side down (if they have skin).
Roast the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until cooked through.
While the salmon is cooking, make the sauce by melting the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the heavy cream and bring to a simmer, scraping the bottom of the pan with a whisk.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 2-3 minutes or until slightly thickened.
During the last 2 minutes of cooking time, stir in the grated cheddar cheese until melted.
Remove the salmon from the oven and let it rest for 2-3 minutes.
Place the salmon on a serving platter or individual plates.
